The treatment is slow, but among the forges there is a long-awaited sense of triumph. The Forge Colossus tears our foes limb from limb as its furnaces burn, and although the stone remains on our bodies, our thoughts are freed from its shackles.With each phase, transformation can be felt within. Though we grudgingly accept our new bodies, we are bound for irreversible change. All has not been to plan, however. Some among us have not withstood the treatment. Their bodies were melted from within by fire, leaving behind nothing but ash. We mourn them, but their wills were perhaps not strong enough for the rites. The ritual priests propose the fallen may have been overwhelmed by a force connected to or perhaps superior to what we're harnessing. A being of pure and ancient fire.Regardless, the Stone Summit will not yield. We are the masters of our lands and all within them.

The Tree Singers were singing until their final seconds—swords burst through chests as their final breaths came out in high, painful notes. Then the Ministry's forces came with fire, which engulfed the Forever Tree. It wasn't necessary—the tree would've died eventually anyway without the Singers' song to sustain it, but the Ministry wanted the tree to die as quickly as possible. Because as the tree started to burn, its Juggernaut, which was still fighting off the Ministry's forces as best it could, staggered. It fell to the ground, weakened, and the Ministry's cowardly forces overwhelmed it, hacking at it with their swords, even though it was already turning to ash with its Forever Tree. The glowing blue light in its chest gradually dimmed until finally it winked out.And then the fire grew out of control, and everyone ran—everyone but me. I stayed and watched as the Forever Tree, and the Juggernaut, and the Tree Singers all burned together, the way they'd lived, their life forces all bound together as one. It was a terrible thing. A terrible thing.

The truth of King Jadon's disappearance and death remains a secret to this day, known only to a privileged few outside of the Collective. Even after Queen Salma reclaimed the throne, the young Mesmer Collective deemed it best that the truth remain buried, worried that Jadon's death under the protection of Ascalonian diplomats would rouse suspicion and undermine the burgeoning trust between the new Ascalonian refugees and the Krytan people.

The Voice of Fate calls upon me  to see the journey of the Unseen  to eternal fruition.  So with tomorrow's sun  dawns a new era.  Anointed by its rays,  we venture forth, three pilgrims  whose destinies entwine to weave  a transcendent tapestry  of the divine.Ulrich returned. His lone communions with the wild rarely last long. He spoke little. And so we went on preparing a meager supper as if nothing had come to pass. We ate mostly in silence. That is, until the fire began to dwindle...Out of the dark, the voice called unto them. For it was they who had been chosen to realize the mission of the Unseen Ones.And thus, it spoke to them, "Chosen three... You are destined... Seek the gate...""As the sun dawns across the wilds... You shall hear my voice... And I will be your guide... You who must open the gate... So fate has written it... So make it be..." To think mere hours ago, our vision was clouded by woes ephemeral. We are so close. Though I can't recall my last full meal, my mind has never felt so sated.

The whispers draw me deeper in. Warm, gentle, like a mother comforting her cub. An old friend welcoming me home. Is this my home now? They remind me I'm still alive, still hungry. Always so hungry. I'm disgusting.I ended a life today. A Vigil patrol. Norn. I don't know why I did it, but it felt wonderful. The whispering friend told me I was doing so well. And as the light drained from his eyes, my stomach growled. My body acted without thinking, and before I knew it, he was gone. I'm vile.What am I becoming? Is it a trick of the light on the snow, or do my claws grow longer? Why does the hunger gnaw away even now, after I did this horrible thing? What's wrong with me? Koda, help me.

Their talk of our fate is an excuse for the corruption they have embraced. We will not be their soldiers. Our transformation does not make us "brethren." We have nothing in common other than our race. They are misguided. They do not represent us.The Summit stands alone at the top.As the days pass, I can feel the stone take hold. Movement takes effort. If this is my fate, I will control it. If we can't undo the Rite, then at least we will use it to our advantage.I have dispatched scouts to key places. We must learn all we can on how to reverse the transformation. It's the only way.

There are few humans in Tyria's history who can say they've challenged a god. Fewer still can claim they defeated one. Yet only one can claim to have ascended our mortal plane, becoming a god herself.Kormir, Goddess of Order, Spirit, and Truth, began her rise to godhood as a decorated Spearmarshal. Even in life, she sought to bring order from chaos, leading the charge against criminals and corsairs who threatened the lives of the innocent.In this exciting collection, Kormir's loyal Sunspear compatriots speak of her mortal years, how she survived her confrontation with the Hunger, the grand battle against the fallen god, Abaddon, and her ultimate ascension to the pantheon.

There is much to be done to heal Caladbolg. In case you are distracted by other matters, here is a list of materials that will be needed eventually:• Collect the remaining shards of Caladbolg.• Collect energy from places of power across Tyria.• Obtain or craft a Vision Crystal to allow you to commune with Caladbolg.• Obtain or craft a Spiritwood Scion to aid in restoring Caladbolg's physical form.• Obtain or craft an Orb of Natural Essence to rejuvenate Caladbolg.If you find yourself in need of guidance, I will remain at your home to provide whatever aid I can.—Ridhais

There is much to be said about the repetitious nature of history. A few hundred years ago, Master Togo was born to Emperor Kintah and his mother, Yuki. While not a legitimate heir, Togo still served Cantha to his fullest and died in that service at the hands of Shiro Tagachi. Today, Lady Joon, a prodigy in her fields of expertise, shines as a beacon of modern Canthan society. An example for the self-made modern woman and an inspiration to the young and inquiring minds of this age.In this dual-biography, two Canthan icons—one of the past and one of the present—are explored in full detail, opening with a transcription of Lady Joon and myself from an interview on TCR, The Canthan Report, that took place last year.Tsui: Lady Joon, thank you for speaking with me today and for your continued support of the Ministry of Archives.Joon: As I say, education—learning is the great equalizer. It's the least I can do.Tsui: Something both you and Master Togo both believed, though perhaps in different ways.Joon: True. Though Xunlai Jade isn't in the business of training storied heroes. Not yet, anyway. (laughs)Tsui: (laughs) Who knows? In my book, I touch on the differences between your early years and Master Togo's early years, and while most can find biographies on Togo at their own leisure, I was wondering if you might share some of your perspective?Joon: (sighs) Let me think. There are the indirect familial connections, of course. Emperor Kintah was fond of Yuki and their son—at least, enough for them to enjoy the privileges of palace life. Emperor Taejeong—my father—did not extend that same...generosity, whatever his reasons.Tsui: And his successor?Joon: (clears throat) Her majesty, Empress Ihn, has shown me a great deal of...consideration, given my achievements through my scientific and business pursuits. We both wish to see Cantha thrive in the modern era, and we both know that means change is inevitable. Politics aside, I am grateful for our relationship.Tsui: Of course. Moving on, what would you define as Master Togo's greatest achievement—his legacy—and what does it mean to you?Joon: A great question. I imagine most probably say it was his sacrifice to protect his half-brother, Emperor Kisu. My answer may disappoint some, but I would have to say his work as a diplomat during the Tengu Wars. His actions during the Tengu Accords prevented what could have been an even greater tragedy. For a time, at least.Tsui: Is that in reference to the actions of the Ministry of Purity?Joon: Who else? I haven't kept tabs on other ministerial efforts to make amends after the civil war, but I have invested some number of resources into outreach efforts. For now, I can say that I employ many tengu at Xunlai Jade, and that their perspectives, experiences, and talents are important not just to me and my business but also to our nation's success.

There were eight of us that left to bring down that extractor. Eight of our finest. We set out at what passes for twilight around here. We stayed low, kept to the shadows, followed all of our covert protocols. I want the record to show that every soldier performed their duties admirably.    I don't know when they spotted us, but they attacked as we rounded the cliff edge. Maja, Genn, and Baiko were sent tumbling into the abyss, their screams swallowed by the void. By the time I had opened the portal, Allene was cut to ribbons and Arni was impaled on one of their claws. I'll remember the look in his eyes as long as I live.    Eight soldiers left that night. Three returned. The extractor still stands.

There were five unscheduled departures from Sanctum Harbor in the early morning hours on the day of the trade summit between Cantha and Elona. I had my technician intercept one of the ship's signals, and we determined that they were manned by two notable Inquest krewes. The ships met with a small fleet of three additional vessels, each of asuran origin, before the signal was blocked.     If you have any information, please touch base with myself or Commodore Marriner.    Signed,  Captain Sayida of the Captain's Council

There were visitors in the village today from Divinity's Reach. Here on holiday, it seems. They expressed shock at the elementals and how comfortable we are with them "But how do you know you can trust them?" they wondered loudly.It's a silly question, though. There are hundreds of armed soldiers patrolling their city, but they never lose sleep over it or wonder why those soldiers are there. Why then should we worry about the elementals? They've protected our village for generations without incident. Garenhoff is as safe and as clean as it is because of them.They're lucky they moved on when they did. I almost gave them a piece of my mind.

These little creatures—the "gwyldell," I believe—are a marvel to study. I'm not sure whether this is due to instinct or if it is a learned behavior, but they've been observed tending to various natural anomalies throughout the forest. They seem to linger near wells of ley energy, and when those wells are disrupted they flock and tend to the flow of magic until it is repaired. I haven't been able to earn their trust, so I've yet to conduct a proper analysis of what they truly are. But given their gentle nature I'd rather they come to me on their own.    I was able to collect a single fecal sample, and after testing it... I sound mad, I know, but I suspect they may be related to treants. Their magical composition is so dense that I'm a bit appalled that they're not more ethereal in nature. It's as if they are manifested from the forest itself, but they are also corporeal. Fascinating.
These notes appear to stem from frustrations about how much time tigers spend napping.
These old bones are caught in the gullet, where laughing rises from sorrow.

These outsiders—Aetherblades, they call themselves—are as arrogant and stupid as Tetra believed them to be. The subject claimed to have been searching for cast-off "technology," but judging from the steam-based devices it carried on its person, they lag far behind Canthan technocrats. Under my supervision, the thief confessed to numerous crimes, ranging from the most petty acts of larceny up to and including the wholesale murder of many innocents during an attack on one of northern Tyria's major cities. Needless to say, I will sleep soundly after this.Through their screams, the subject was quite insistent about their desire to return to their homelands, though the subject made the ludicrous claim that they could traverse the Mists with the same ease that a skiff parts the waters of Lake Lutgardgis. The subject claimed to be searching for a specific part that would help them in that mission.Before we could pry out any more details, Tetra ordered me to stop. She told me that the pirate band's leader, an individual going by Ankka, had approached her with a proposal. We assumed she was after her lackey, but no...she was only interested in the technology that was on the subject. While Tetra was busy considering the terms of the deal, the pirates simply...withdrew from negotiations.Tetra was not pleased by their sudden change of heart, though I am skeptical that the scoundrels would have upheld any deal we might have brokered. For my part, I'm happy enough with how things have gone, as it gives me more time with my subject. I believe there are many more gems to uncover in this one.

These pirates are uncultured and filthy. These are not the working conditions to achieve great technological advancement!  Our "friends" know nothing of the subtleties and elegance of synergetics. Not only are our research specimens delicate and to be handled with the most gentle touch, but my lab equipment must be treated with the utmost respect (the pirates do neither).  B is arranging a visit to my humble lab in the coming week. I haven't had the pleasure of meeting her myself yet, but I want this place spotless by the time she arrives. The force-field generator above the power control core has been malfunctioning, I should get that repaired before her arrival. That way, I can give our guest a full demonstration of the lab's ability.

These scrolls are filled with logistical data from 1689 CC, and they seem to imply that the Ministry of Purity used accusations of sedition to concentrate wealth into the hands of their strongest supporters. A short foreword is written on the collection, in a different hand:"They're on to us. My sources say at least three of our sites have been compromised, possibly more. We need to be more careful.These are the most critical records my followers have collected: I've enclosed copies for you in case my group is next. We need to isolate ourselves so that one discovery won't collapse the whole network.This will be the last time I contact you. May we meet again, in a brighter future."

Thess's Ward
They are up to no good.

They brought in another group of refugees today. As unsettling as the Kryptis are to look at, you adjust to that when you start talking to them, as they're surprisingly intelligent.Normally, that is. But today's batch was all these little ones, just a flood of teeth and claws. When we set out food for them, they tore through it so aggressively that I genuinely worried they would eat the table as well. When they were done, it was as though a swarm of locusts had passed by, picking every last scrap of anything even resembling food.They thanked us afterward though, in that same awkward way that they say everything else. Once the hunger was gone, there was only gratitude. They had been certain they were going to die, and now they follow us around the camp like rescued pups. I can't help but feel sorry for them.
They did say they'd be paying us, right?

They gave us their best fight, but we were able to take them both down. Horrik didn't make it out alive, but Mai Trin wisely surrendered to Kiel before we diced her into skritt feed.With Mai Trin in custody, maybe we'll figure out who's behind this whole mess.

They hylek have finally realized the danger they've put themselves in by giving Zhaitan more fodder for its undead army. Now they must confront that danger as the Risen horde descends on their village.
They may look like big chickens, but dragons are not for eating.
They needed to hold out a little longer—buy enough time for their partner to gain control of the cannon. They knew this dance all too well, and it was always exhilarating.
They never come back whole. Maybe they're only half-remembered...
They ran off to a secluded glen in Fields of Ruin. It's near Tenaebron Waypoint.
They ran off to Bloodtide Coast to look for high ground on a secluded island southeast of Barrier Camp Waypoint.
They ran off to Bloodtide Coast, into a hidden grove a bit north of Lostwreck Waypoint.
They ran off to Crystal Oasis, mumbling something about wanting to learn to become master chefs.
They ran off to Desert Highlands to hang out with the choya of Pricklepatch Hollow. To each their own.
They ran off to Desert Highlands, but we couldn't follow them without a jackal. They said something about a castle in the sky...?
They ran off to Diessa Plateau to pick strawberries near the Town of Nolan.
They ran off to Domain of Istan. You'll find them somewhere in the Astralarium.
They ran off to Domain of Kourna. You'll find them overlooking a choya idol.
They ran off to Dragon's End...while mumbling the word "fox" over and over again.
They ran off to Ember Bay. They said something about...joining the circus?
They ran off to Frostgorge Sound. You'll find them somewhere in Shattered Ice Floe.
They ran off to Grothmar Valley to pester Metal Legion for autographs between sets.
They ran off to Lake Doric. You'll find them overlooking Noran's Homestead.
They ran off to look at puppies near the Village of Astorea in Caledon Forest.
They ran off to Lornar's Pass to knock at Grenth's Door.
They ran off to New Kaineng City to try to live it up among the aristocrats in some duck-themed teahouse.
They ran off to Queensdale, and they're somewhere in Demongrub Pits.
They ran off to Seitung Province. Look for them near a shrine on an island of cats.
They ran off to Siren's Landing. You'll find them high above Pilaster Rock.
They ran off to Snowden Drifts. It seems like they're friends with Durgar?
They ran off to some kind of Mists displacement in Jahai Bluffs. That figures.
They ran off to someplace near Ojon's Lumbermill in Queensdale.
They ran off to Tarir, the Forgotten City, in Auric Basin. They must be somewhere in the Inner Chamber.
They ran off to the Desolation, into a place in the Silent Vale that's still green.
They ran off to unwind in Seitung Province. Check the small island in Seitung Harbor.
They ran off to Verarium Delves in Sparkfly Fen. You may have to do some jumping and climbing to find them.
They ran off to Verdant Brink to listen to some live lute music.
They ran off to Verdant Brink—somewhere near Forsaken Thicket. Guess it felt safer there than deeper in the wilds.
They ran off to...wait. They never left Lion's Arch? They're just hiding over on the west side of the harbor!
They roam where the spirit is honored.
They said "Watch the ledges," so we did. It's a nice view despite seeing the chaos in the Tundra when that ice dragon was around.
They sure did put a lot of defenses in their library, huh?
They walked through the Underworld, dove straight into the Mystic Forge. They crossed through the long-forgotten halls of Ahdashim—such feats would be cast in gold and told to awe-struck children.

They will protect the veil between Tyria and the Mists.    They will guide the Astral Ward toward brilliance.    They will be modest, and they will drink a chalice of water from the Golden Lake.    They will be kind and powerful.     They, by the will of Isgarren, will better this world.
They're effective against shield generators.
They're effective against the shield generators.
They're hatching!
They're really not going to offer us anything permanent, are they?

They've pushed the naga all the way out to the bay, though that fight is long from finished. I don't know what "Minister" Jimin expected, pushing them from their homes so we could build ours. After everything that we've experienced, you'd think there'd be a trickle of empathy left in his heart.Emperor Bitgaram's health is weakening. The stress from the disaster—coupled with his advanced age—has made unifying Cantha even more challenging. He has left New Kaineng and is resting in the royal family's estate in Shing Jea. Haebaragi is leading our efforts here in his stead. Her vision is much aligned with mine, pushing Jimin to ease up on the naga as they flee to the west.She wants to rebuild the Kaineng docks here—turn it into a new hub of trade for the nation. I've even heard whispers of reopening the borders, but that idea is still widely contested by the Ministry of Purity. Though, even they are falling out of fashion these days.Minister Doh Taelin, Ministry of Archives, 1737 CC
